settings.title: Now Pouring: Beans from Hillcrest Roastery, Three Streets Over
settings.excerpt: We have partnered with a neighbourhood roaster so your house cup never travels far.
settings.body:
Good coffee should not need a passport. Starting this month, our house blend comes from Hillcrest Roastery, a small operation just three streets away.

Roasting nearby means the beans reach us days off the roast rather than weeks, and it keeps our money in the neighbourhood. We think you will taste the difference in the cup.

Why we changed roasters

For a long time our house beans travelled a long way to get to us, and by the time we opened a bag the brightest flavours had already faded. Coffee is at its best in the first couple of weeks after roasting, and we were rarely brewing inside that window. Working with Hillcrest fixes that. We collect the beans by hand most weeks, often within a day or two of the roast date stamped on the bag.

It also means we can talk to the people who roast our coffee. When we wanted the house blend to sit a little sweeter for milk drinks, we walked over and asked, and they adjusted the next batch. That kind of back-and-forth is not possible with a supplier on the other side of the country.

What this means for your cup

  • Fresher beans, usually brewed within two weeks of roasting.
  • A blend tuned with our bar in mind, not a generic catalogue option.
  • Money that stays close to home and supports a neighbour.

Come try the new house blend on espresso or filter, and let us know what you think. We are still dialling it in across both brew methods, and your notes genuinely help us settle it.
